doc:
Pure-TS port of MPFR's mpfr_roundeven: round an MPFR value to the nearest integer with ties-to-EVEN (RNDN semantics), at the caller-supplied prec, returning {value, ternary}. The C body (mpfr/src/rint.c L308-L312) is a one-line delegation: mpfr_roundeven(r, u) := mpfr_rint(r, u, MPFR_RNDN). There is NO rnd parameter; the direction is implicit (nearest, ties to even). Contrast with mpfr_round (sibling in the same file, L317-L320) which delegates to MPFR_RNDNA (ties-AWAY): roundeven(2.5)=2 and roundeven(0.5)=0, whereas round(2.5)=3 and round(0.5)=1. Class is 'misc' (50ms per-case budget): integer extraction plus a prec-fit rounding step, both O(prec) bigint shifts; no transcendental complexity.

The engine mpfr_rint (rint.c L35-L304) handles three regimes:
  1. singular u (NaN/Inf/Zero): NaN -> NaN (ternary 0); +-Inf -> +-Inf (sign preserved, ternary 0); +-0 -> +-0 (signed zero preserved, ternary 0).
  2. 0 < |u| < 1 (exp <= 0): rounds |u| to 0 or 1. For RNDN the 0.5 boundary rounds to 0 (rint.c L82: 'in the MPFR_RNDN mode, 0.5 must be rounded to 0'); strictly-greater-than-0.5 rounds away to +-1. mpfr_powerof2_raw(u) detects the exact-0.5 case.
  3. |u| >= 1 (exp > 0): extracts the integer part, then applies ties-to-even rounding when prec is smaller than the integer's bit-width.

Ternary semantics: ternary = sign(rounded - exact). mpfr_rint returns +-uflags where uflags in {0,1,2}; the +-2 (from the exp<=0 branch, L91/L96) is normalised to +-1 on the wire by jl_output_result. uflags==0 means u was a representable integer (exact, ternary 0).
divergence_from_c:
1. Return shape: C mutates r (which carries prec) and returns int ternary; TS takes prec as a positional argument and returns {value, ternary} per the locked Result schema in src/core.ts.
2. No rnd parameter: the function name fixes the mode to ties-to-even (RNDN). The TS port must NOT accept a rnd argument; the golden inputs are {x, prec} only.
3. Ternary magnitude: the C ternary can be +-2 (mpfr_rint returns +-uflags, uflags in {0,1,2}); the wire normalises to {-1,0,1} via jl_output_result, and the TS port must return a Ternary in {-1,0,1} (sign of rounded - exact).
4. Flag side effects (overflow/underflow on the |u|<1 set_si path and on the carry-past-emax path) are not surfaced in the Result; the returned value is still correctly rounded. Under the default exponent range these special exponent paths are not reached by the golden (no emin/emax narrowing in the driver).

Application notes:
  - signature.params: [x, prec] aligns with mpfr_round (the structural sibling at rint.c L317).
  - class: 'misc' (rint.c basename family); 50ms budget.
  - prec_unit: 'bits'.
refs: mpfr/src/rint.c L308-L312 -- mpfr_roundeven wrapper: return mpfr_rint(r, u, MPFR_RNDN).

mpfr/src/rint.c L35-L304 -- mpfr_rint engine; exp<=0 branch (L80-L98) handles |u|<1 and uses mpfr_powerof2_raw to detect the exact-0.5 boundary; the RNDN ties-to-even logic is at L156-L185 and L241-L271.
mpfr/src/rint.c L317-L320 -- mpfr_round (RNDNA, ties-AWAY) sibling; the contrast that makes the roundeven adversarial ties cases discriminating.
mpfr/tests/trint.c L36-L110 -- special() + coverage tests driving mpfr_rint(.,.,MPFR_RNDN); mined source.
src/core.ts -- locked MPFR shape, Result, RoundingMode, Ternary, MPFRError.
src/ops/rint.ts -- the RNDN-mode engine the port should delegate to.
src/ops/round.ts -- RNDNA sibling (ties-away) with shared scaffolding.
CLAUDE.md 'Hallucination-risk callouts' -- NaN!=NaN; signed zero is real; ternary is sign of (rounded - exact); RNDN is ties-to-EVEN (0.5 -> 0, 2.5 -> 2).
